adrs.title: Canonical registry is a single JSON document
Features are the targetable units
Issues are plannable work items
Entity-centric registry with a derived graph export
Normalized prefixed IDs
Claim status and claim tier are orthogonal
Feature implementation state is separate from lifecycle state
Immutable boundary and release snapshots
Fail-closed guards
Generated projections are non-canonical
Explicit schema versioning
Portable SSOT core with repo-specific evidence adapters
Enforce max path and filename lengths under `.ssot`
Profiles are reusable feature bundles with derived pass status
Monorepo package topology under `pkgs/`
Contracts, views, and codegen split
Generated artifacts are derived and non-canonical
Python runtime remains the canonical behavior engine
Cross-language parity strategy
GitHub Actions multi-package release orchestration
Hybrid package versioning policy
Schema-owned max length for normalized ids
ssot-contracts is the sole packaged schema authority
Workspace package-dependency DAG
Govern migration paths as explicit schema edges
Document schema_version in README managed badges
Assurance-language ceilings for feature target claim tiers
Adopt RFC 8785 JCS for .ssot JSON artifacts
CLI list ids filtering

status_notes.note: Accepted with automated status sync implementation

adrs.title: Automated registry status synchronization
Canonical registry is a single JSON document
Features are the targetable units
Issues are plannable work items
Entity-centric registry with a derived graph export
Normalized prefixed IDs
Claim status and claim tier are orthogonal
Feature implementation state is separate from lifecycle state
Immutable boundary and release snapshots
Fail-closed guards
Generated projections are non-canonical
Explicit schema versioning
Portable SSOT core with repo-specific evidence adapters
Enforce max path and filename lengths under `.ssot`
Profiles are reusable feature bundles with derived pass status
SPECs own typed ADR links
Downstream assurance-language ceilings for feature target claim tiers

claims.description: Migration coverage stays aligned with the entity-id max-length contract.
claims.title: Migration entity id max-length rollout claim

claims.description: Normalized ids stay within the shared max-length contract.
claims.title: Schema entity id max-length claim

claims.description: SPEC rows and authored SPEC payloads support typed adr_ids with validated ADR targets.
claims.title: Schema SPEC adr_ids claim

claims.description: Document APIs persist, validate, and link typed SPEC to ADR relationships.
claims.title: API SPEC ADR linking claim

claims.description: SPEC CLI flows expose typed --adr-ids flags for create, update, link, and unlink operations.
claims.title: CLI flag --adr-ids claim

claims.description: Upgrade flow adds adr_ids defaults to registry SPEC rows and authored SPEC payloads.
claims.title: Migration SPEC adr_ids rollout claim

claims.description: Graph export emits explicit SPEC to ADR edges and derived feature to ADR visibility without storing feature adr_ids.
claims.title: Graph SPEC ADR visibility claim

claims.description: Every known non-current schema_version has a verified migration route to the current schema_version.
claims.title: Migration paths are fully covered

claims.description: README schema_version and migration coverage badges are generated from current code constants and migration path inventory.
claims.title: README schema_version badges are generated

claims.description: Renaming an entity id preserves all inbound references and active program pointers.
claims.title: Entity ID rename preserves linked identity

claims.description: ssot-core writes and validates .ssot JSON artifacts using RFC 8785 JCS canonical JSON rules.
claims.title: JCS canonical JSON is enforced for .ssot artifacts

claims.description: Entity list subcommands return requested ids and reject unknown ids.
claims.title: Entity list surfaces filter by ids

claims.description: ADR/SPEC list subcommands return requested ids and reject unknown ids.
claims.title: Document list surfaces filter by ids

claims.description: The registry provides a dry-run capable automated status synchronization command and API.
claims.title: Automated status sync is implemented
